title: Right Side View
description:
### Objective
Return the values of the nodes visible from the right side of a binary tree when looking from top to bottom.

### Function Contract
Implement `__init__(self: any, val: any, left: any, right: any)` in Python.
Do not change input/output semantics expected by the tests.

### Input and Output
- Input `tree`: type `array` (example: `[1,2,3,null,5,null,4]`).
- Return type: `array`.

### Requirements
- Return the exact expected output for every test case.
- Handle edge cases (empty input, duplicates, boundary values) without raising exceptions.
- Target complexity: time `O(n)`, space `O(w)`.

### Constraints
- 0 ≤ nodes ≤ 10^5
explanation:
Intuition
At each level, the last node dequeued in BFS is the visible one from the right.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Perform BFS by levels
2. Main Algorithm: Record the last node on each level
3. Return Result: Return the collected list

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(w)

Key Insights
- Level order naturally groups nodes by depth
- The final node at a level is the rightmost

Common Pitfalls
- Recording the first instead of last flips the view
- Mixing levels without size tracking
